channel.title: The Grant Desk - Visual & Media Arts, Worldwide
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Visual & Media Arts, Worldwide.

item.title: Flickr x Black Women Photographers Grant 2026 (Traces of Connection)
item.description:
Organisation: Flickr and Black Women Photographers (BWP)

Location: Open internationally to Black female and non-binary photographers; winner announced at the MODE by Flickr festival in Minneapolis, USA (18-20 September 2026).

Award: USD 10,000 grant to invest in the recipient's practice, plus a two-year Flickr Pro and one-year SmugMug Pro membership; ten additional recipients each receive one-year Flickr Pro and SmugMug Pro memberships. No entry fee (BWP membership is free).

Duration: Winner announced live at MODE by Flickr, 18-20 September 2026

Deadline: 06 Aug 2026

Flickr and Black Women Photographers award a USD 10,000 grant to a Black female or non-binary photographer to invest in their practice. The 2026 edition centers on the theme Traces of Connection, chosen by 2025 recipient and guest judge Eleonore Menga. TO APPLY: be a member of the Black Women Photographers organization (free), join the BWP Flickr Community, submit one photograph inspired by the theme to the community, and share a brief description of how the image connects to it. The winner also receives a two-year Flickr Pro and a one-year SmugMug Pro membership, and is announced live at the first MODE by Flickr festival in Minneapolis (18-20 September 2026); ten additional recipients receive Flickr Pro and SmugMug Pro memberships. DEADLINE: 6 August 2026. No entry fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Liu Shiming Art Foundation: Artist Grants 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Liu Shiming Art Foundation

Location: Open to artists of any country; application must be in English.

Award: Up to five grants of USD 5,000 each. Disbursed in two payments (USD 3,000 by 15 January 2027 and USD 2,000 on project completion). No application fee.

Duration: Project grant for new work

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

The Liu Shiming Art Foundation's annual Artist Grants (fourth edition) award up to five grants of USD 5,000 each to early-career visual artists to create new work exploring connections between traditional, cultural, or historical influences and contemporary artistic practice. ELIGIBILITY: visual artists in the first decade of their career (two to ten years practicing professionally); applicants must have received a studio-arts degree between 2016 and 2024, or had their debut gallery or juried-exhibition show between 2016 and 2026. Open to artists of any country; the application and all supporting materials must be in English. TIMELINE: applications open 1 June 2026; close 31 August 2026; recipients notified by 2 December 2026. Apply via the Foundation's grants page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Akademie Schloss Solitude: Web Residencies No. 22 - Ignore All Previous Instructions
item.description:
Organisation: Akademie Schloss Solitude

Location: Online (fully remote). Awarded by Akademie Schloss Solitude, Stuttgart, Germany.

Award: EUR 1,200 grant for a four-week online residency, plus a microsite to develop and publish the project, and support from a web/UX designer and a programmer in a supportive capacity.

Duration: Four-week online residency.

Deadline: 07 Aug 2026

Web Residencies No. 22, 'Ignore All Previous Instructions,' is curated by !Mediengruppe Bitnik as part of Akademie Schloss Solitude's Web Residencies program, which since 2016 has supported young talents from the international digital scene as well as artists of all disciplines engaging with web-based practices. For the tenth anniversary of the program, this edition invites artists to create works that challenge extractive digital platforms and AI systems, exploring how to interrupt dominant technological logics, from ad-tech surveillance to prompt injection vulnerabilities, and imagine alternative structures and communication methods. The open call seeks proposals that hijack, glitch, and disrupt rather than comply with existing systems, drawing on strategies like Situationist detournement to map routes through and around the grids we are trapped within. Four to six proposals will be selected. BENEFITS: a four-week online residency; a grant of EUR 1,200; a web space (microsite) on which the project can be developed and published online; support from a web/UX designer and a programmer (in a supportive capacity only, not to build an entire website); and participation in individual and group online events with the curators and Solitude fellows. Offline or hybrid formats (a reading session, panel, exhibition, or curated program) may follow. ELIGIBILITY: artists of all disciplines may apply, with no age limit; submission is free of charge. The residency takes place exclusively online, and selected fellows agree to be available online during the four weeks. TIMELINE: the open call runs from July 7 to August 7, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Prospect Art: NEW WORK Commission 2027 - "We Come in Pieces"
item.description:
Organisation: Prospect Art

Location: Remote / online. Open to visual artists worldwide; programs (ONE WORK, BROADCAST) run online.

Award: NEW WORK: USD 1,500 commission for one artist, plus a featured website section and social promotion. BROADCAST: USD 125 screening or speaking fee if selected. ONE WORK: a published article (exposure). NOTE: a USD 10 administrative fee applies to apply, with a no-questions-asked waiver available on request before the deadline. PayPal account required to receive funds.

Duration: The commissioned NEW WORK project should be completed within twelve months of selection.

Deadline: 16 Aug 2026

Prospect Art invites visual artists worldwide to apply for the 2027 NEW WORK commission, a USD 1,500 project grant supporting the creation of a new, conceptually driven work responding to the curatorial theme "We Come in Pieces," developed by 2026-2027 Curatorial Fellow Alma Sammel. Through a single application, artists are also considered for ONE WORK (an online series featuring an in-depth conversation centered on a single completed artwork) and BROADCAST (a curated online program of screenings, artist talks, and discussions). THEME: "We Come in Pieces" proposes the Alien as a method for shifting perspective, approaching alienness as a practice of estrangement to encounter the world beyond familiar human-centered narratives. It explores relationships between human and more-than-human worlds, speculative cosmologies, ecological systems, technologies, landscapes, histories, infrastructures, and nonhuman forms of intelligence (satellites and orbital debris, fungal networks, oceans, atmospheric systems, algorithmic intelligences), inviting works that displace the point of listening, seeing, sensing, or knowing. Research-based and conceptually driven practices are especially encouraged. WHAT'S OFFERED: NEW WORK, USD 1,500 to one artist plus a featured website section and social promotion (project completed within 12 months); ONE WORK, a published article and social promotion; BROADCAST, a USD 125 screening or speaking fee and inclusion in a themed program. ELIGIBILITY: professional visual artists working in any medium at any career stage, except those currently enrolled in undergraduate or graduate studio art programs; collectives may apply. Media welcomed include moving image, installation, sculpture, photography, performance, sound, digital media, social practice, and interdisciplinary forms. APPLICATION: submit via the official Google Form with contact information, a project description (250 words), a sketch/visualization (1-3 images), up to 3 links to completed work samples, a statement relating the project to the theme (200 words), a short bio and exhibition history (500 words), and a budget breakdown of the USD 1,500. A USD 10 administrative fee is paid via PayPal (include PayPal name and transaction ID); a fee waiver is available with no explanation by emailing submissions@prospectart.org before the deadline. Writers may email gioj@prospectart.org with samples. DEADLINE: 16 August 2026; results for the NEW WORK grant notified by end of September. INFO SESSION: 5 August 2026 at noon PST.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: New Emergence Art Prize 2026
item.description:
Organisation: New Emergence Art

Location: International; open worldwide. Shortlist shown in an online exhibition.

Award: Prize fund worth over GBP 5,000 across nine awards, including a GBP 1,000 Grand Prize cash award, a two-week NG Art Creative Residency in Provence, France (worth EUR 2,000), a GBP 300 Founder's Prize cash award, and materials or subscription awards from Staedtler UK, Rosemary & Co, GreatArt, Michael Harding, and The Art Ladder. NOTE: there is a paid entry fee.

Duration: One-off prize; shortlisted artists shown in an online exhibition 11 August to 7 September 2026

Deadline: 03 Aug 2026

The New Emergence Art Prize 2026 is an international open call for emerging artists, presented by New Emergence Art. There is no set theme and work may be submitted in any medium. AWARDS: a prize fund worth over GBP 5,000 across nine awards supporting artists through cash prizes, artist materials, mentoring, and a residency, including a GBP 1,000 Grand Prize, a two-week NG Art Creative Residency in Provence, France (worth EUR 2,000), a GBP 300 Founder's Prize cash award, and materials or subscription awards from Staedtler UK, Rosemary & Co, GreatArt, Michael Harding, and The Art Ladder. ELIGIBILITY: open internationally to emerging artists; entrants may submit up to three original artworks that represent the quality, originality and direction of their practice. Final selections are made by an independent judging panel considering concept, execution, originality and impact. Shortlisted artists are featured in an online exhibition running 11 August to 7 September 2026. NOTE: there is a paid entry fee (amount set at the entry step). DEADLINE: 3 August 2026, 23:59 BST. Apply via https://www.newemergenceart.com/apply.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Pollock-Krasner Foundation Grant for Artists
item.description:
Organisation: Pollock-Krasner Foundation

Location: New York, USA (grants awarded internationally)

Award: Up to USD 50,000 (unrestricted)

Duration: One-year grant period

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The Pollock-Krasner Foundation awards unrestricted grants to professional visual artists who are painters, sculptors, and artists working on paper, including printmakers. Grants range up to USD 50,000 over a one-year period and can be used for both professional and personal expenses such as art supplies, studio rent, and living costs. Applicants must demonstrate recognizable artistic merit and financial need, and must be actively exhibiting their current work in professional venues such as galleries and museums. The Foundation does not fund students, commercial or graphic art, video, performance, film, or generative and AI-generated work. Applications are accepted on a rolling, year-round basis with no deadline, and the review process typically takes nine months to a year. There is no application fee, and all applications must be submitted online.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Adolph and Esther Gottlieb Foundation Emergency Grant
item.description:
Organisation: Adolph and Esther Gottlieb Foundation

Location: New York, USA (applicants worldwide)

Award: Up to USD 15,000 (USD 5,000 typical), one-time grant

Duration: One-time emergency grant

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The Adolph and Esther Gottlieb Emergency Grant provides interim financial assistance to painters, sculptors, and printmakers facing an unforeseen, catastrophic event such as fire, flood, or a medical emergency. Applicants must have been engaged in a mature phase of their art for at least ten years and must have a current, urgent need that is beyond their means. The maximum award is USD 15,000, with USD 5,000 being typical, given as one-time assistance for a specific emergency. The program does not cover dental work, chronic situations, capital improvements, projects, general indebtedness, or lack of employment. There is no deadline; applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, usually within about four weeks, and within 24 to 48 hours in the most urgent cases.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Innovate Grant
item.description:
Organisation: Innovate Artist Grants

Location: Online (applications accepted worldwide)

Award: USD 1,800 unrestricted grant (two awarded per cycle: one to a visual artist, one to a photographer) USD 35 application fee

Duration: One-time unrestricted award; quarterly cycles (Winter, Spring, Summer, Fall)

Deadline: 17 Sep 2026

Innovate Grant awards two unrestricted USD 1,800 grants every quarter, one to a visual artist and one to a photographer, along with up to 18 honorable mentions who are featured and promoted on the organization's website. The award is unrestricted, so recipients can apply the funds toward new projects, materials, studio rent, travel, or other needs. Grant cycles run four times a year (Winter, Spring, Summer, and Fall). Visual artists and photographers aged 18 and older from anywhere in the world, both US and international, are eligible to apply. Applications require contact details, a grant category, and 3 to 10 images of work, plus a USD 35 application fee. The Spring 2026 cycle closed on June 18, 2026, and the Summer 2026 cycle follows on the regular quarterly schedule.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: TED & POSCA Global Artist Residency 2026: Visible Mending
item.description:
Organisation: TED & POSCA (curated by Fine Acts)

Location: Globally distributed; no single location or central studio. Selected artists work within their own communities, with projects unfolding in public space.

Award: Each selected artist receives a total of USD 15,000 (covering artist fees, production costs/community activation, and documentation), plus a production budget and tools from POSCA, creative direction and storytelling support, and visibility through TED and Fine Acts' global platforms.

Duration: One-year residency term; community-rooted public interventions developed in 2026 for implementation in 2027. The initiative runs for multiple years (2027, 2028, 2029), expanding the cohort year by year.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

TED (the global platform for ideas and storytelling) and POSCA (a creative brand by uni Brands Corporation) are launching a new kind of artist residency: a globally distributed cohort of artists working across different geographies, cultures, and communities, united by a shared drive to shape public life through art. Fine Acts, the external curatorial partner selected by TED & POSCA, leads the curation, creative direction, and coordination. There is no single location and no central studio: selected artists work within their own communities, following a shared creative constraint (a unifying conceptual framework around repair, hope, and beauty), with projects unfolding in public space, community participation as a key part of the work, and process mattering as much as outcome. 2026 THEME, VISIBLE MENDING: inspired in part by Kintsugi (the Japanese practice of repairing broken ceramics with gold), the theme explores repair as a creative and civic act, inviting artists to consider how wounds, ruptures, and imperfections can become sites of transformation, and to make processes of repair visible through public interventions that spark reflection, dialogue, and collective imagination. In 2026, four artists will be selected to develop community-rooted public interventions to be implemented in 2027. AWARD: each selected artist receives a total of USD 15,000 to cover artist fees, production costs/community activation, and documentation, plus a production budget and tools from POSCA, creative direction and storytelling support, and visibility through TED and Fine Acts' global platforms. While the residency term is one year, the initiative will run for multiple years (2027, 2028, 2029), expanding the cohort year by year and building an evolving global archive of civic artistic interventions. Apply by 31 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Club Nothing: Community Music Fund
item.description:
Organisation: Nothing

Location: Anywhere in the world.

Award: USD 40,000 fund total; four individuals or collectives each receive USD 10,000 to produce a club event, series, or festival. No application fee.

Duration: Event, series, or festival to take place later in 2026; winners announced late August 2026.

Deadline: 09 Aug 2026

Club Nothing is a global events series and USD 40,000 fund from the tech brand Nothing, supporting organisers, artists, and collectives creating new music experiences. The fund will award four individuals or collectives USD 10,000 each to throw a club event, series, or festival later this year, backing disruptors bringing new sounds and experiences to their communities through nightlife anywhere in the world. Applications are judged by a panel including DJ and producer Manuka Honey (Marissa Malik), Nothing Global Senior Community Manager and Madruga Festival co-founder Erica Travis, Nothing Global Head of Communications Shavone Charles, Dazed Music Editor Solomon Pace-McCarrick, A&R and music-publishing founder James Supreme, and music-industry professional Layla Mustafa Rock. Applications close 9 August 2026; winners announced late August 2026. Learn more and apply at nothing.tech/club-nothing (details also posted at https://nothing.community/d/59214-want-10000-to-throw-a-party). See full terms at nothing.tech/club-nothing/terms-and-conditions.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Eamonn McCabe Bursary 2026 (Royal Photographic Society)
item.description:
Organisation: The Royal Photographic Society (RPS), in partnership with Eamonn McCabe's family and The Guardian

Location: International bursary. Applicants must be aged 30 years or under on the closing date. Interviews held via Zoom.

Award: GBP 3,000 to deliver a photography project on the theme of 'sporting endeavour'. The completed project will be published by The Guardian and the RPS, who will work with the recipient to bring the project to a wider audience.

Duration: The project should be completed within one year of being awarded.

Deadline: 03 Aug 2026

The Eamonn McCabe Bursary, launched in 2024, commemorates the life of photojournalist Eamonn McCabe (1948-2022), who made his name as a sports photographer before moving to wider editorial work, mainly for The Observer and The Guardian, and who supported a generation of photographers from the 1980s to the 2020s. Run by the RPS in partnership with McCabe's family and The Guardian, this international bursary continues that legacy. AWARD: GBP 3,000 to a photographer aged 30 years or under to deliver a photography project responding to the theme of 'sporting endeavour', which can be interpreted as widely as the applicant wishes, though the general approach should be suitable for publication. The project should normally be new, not previously exhibited or published. As well as professional sports, the call particularly encourages projects covering grassroots and community activities (for example, addressing issues such as mental health or supporting displaced communities), and welcomes creative interpretations of the theme. The completed project will be published by The Guardian and the RPS. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be aged 30 years or under on the closing date. PROCESS: interviews via Zoom in September; recipient announced by the end of September; project completed within one year of award. Apply via the RPS application portal using RPS Account credentials. Closing date 3 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Textile Exchange x Atmos: Fashion Regenerated 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Textile Exchange (with Atmos)

Location: Open call; work to be promoted globally through Textile Exchange and Atmos and exhibited at the 2026 Textile Exchange conference.

Award: Overall winner: a paid collaborative commission worth USD 10,000 with Textile Exchange to develop a new body of work documenting the evidence of regeneration, published through Atmos and Textile Exchange and shown at the 2026 conference. Two runners-up: work promoted by Textile Exchange and displayed at the conference exhibition, plus a USD 2,000 cash prize.

Duration: One-off commission/prize.

Deadline: Closed 20 Jul 2026

Fashion Regenerated is an open call from Textile Exchange and Atmos. Winners are selected by a panel of expert judges and, for the first time, a public vote. The shortlist is promoted through Textile Exchange and Atmos, amplifying inspirational stories to an engaged audience of thousands. The overall winner receives a paid collaborative commission to the value of USD 10,000 with Textile Exchange to develop a new body of work documenting the evidence of regeneration, working closely with the team to identify the right story and access; the winning work is published through Atmos, Textile Exchange, and shown to an audience of more than 1,300 changemakers at the 2026 Textile Exchange conference. Two runners-up have their work promoted by Textile Exchange and displayed at the conference exhibition, and there is also a USD 2,000 cash prize. Check the call site for full eligibility and submission details. Deadline: 20 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Foundwork Artist Prize 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Foundwork

Location: Open call. No location restriction stated; submitted online via Foundwork.

Award: The honoree receives an unrestricted USD 10,000 grant, studio visits with each of the five jurors, and an interview for Foundwork's Dialogues program. Three artists named to the Short List each receive a USD 1,000 micro-grant.

Duration: One-off annual juried award.

Deadline: 25 Sep 2026

The Foundwork Artist Prize is an annual juried award supporting outstanding artistic practices. The 2026 honoree receives an unrestricted USD 10,000 grant, studio visits with each of the five jurors, and an interview for Foundwork's Dialogues program. In addition, three artists are named to the 2026 Short List, each receiving a USD 1,000 micro-grant in support of their practice. Submissions are made online through Foundwork. Application deadline 25 September 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Artivism & Amplifiers Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Human Impacts Institute

Location: Global; pairs young climate leaders from the Global North and Global South. Open to speakers of English, Spanish, French, and Portuguese.

Award: USD 1,000 stipend upon completion. Supported by expert workshops and creative mentors.

Duration: Six-month program.

Deadline: Closed 01 Jul 2026

The Artivism & Amplifiers (A&A) Fellowship pairs young climate leaders from the Global North and Global South to co-create multimedia campaigns, exhibits, and installations for the Creative Climate Awards 2027. Over six months, fellows build skills in intercultural collaboration, climate storytelling, and community engagement, supported by expert workshops and creative mentors. The program is open to speakers of English, Spanish, French, and Portuguese. Fellows receive a USD 1,000 stipend upon completion. The organizers strongly encourage applications from people most impacted by the climate crisis, including those from the global majority, Indigenous, LGBTQIA+, immigrant, and diaspora communities, and people with disabilities. Applications close Wednesday 1 July 2026 at 9am GMT+2.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: OIF: Support for Artist Mobility and Circulation of Cultural Goods 2026 (Francophonie)
item.description:
Organisation: Organisation internationale de la Francophonie (OIF)

Location: Francophone zone (OIF member states globally)

Award: Up to EUR 5,000 for artist mobility (travel, accommodation, visas, logistics). Up to EUR 2,500 for circulation of cultural goods (excess baggage, freight, customs). Both can be combined. No application fee.

Duration: Residencies or tours of 5 days to 3 months. Projects must take place between 25 March and 30 November 2026.

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

The OIF Support for Artist Mobility and Circulation of Cultural Goods programme provides grants of up to EUR 5,000 for mobility (travel, accommodation, visa fees, logistics) and up to EUR 2,500 for circulation of cultural goods (excess baggage, freight, customs) within the Francophone zone; both can be combined. ELIGIBILITY: professional artists and established artistic collectives with a demonstrated connection to the Francophone space (by nationality, working language, or artistic collaborations), residing in an OIF member state, with minimum 2 years of experience in the cultural or artistic sector; must directly manage the project (not act as intermediaries); one application per individual per call. ELIGIBLE DISCIPLINES: live performance (music, theatre, dance, comedy, storytelling), cinema/audiovisual, literature/publishing, and visual arts. PRIORITY given to projects promoting gender equality, women artists, creators under 35, and South-South mobility. Projects must take place between 25 March and 30 November 2026. Multiple selection sessions throughout the year; call open until 31 August 2026. Information webinars: 11 March, 13 May, and 8 July 2026 at 3 PM Paris time. Contact: culture.dlc@francophonie.org. Apply at https://appelsaprojets.francophonie.org/fr/applications/soutien-a-la-circulation-des-biens-et-mobilite-des-artistes.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Lee Ufan Arles: Prix Art & Environnement 2026 (Residency + Exhibition Prize)
item.description:
Organisation: Lee Ufan Arles and Maison Guerlain

Location: Arles, France (residency and exhibition)

Award: Funded residency (production space + accommodation) in Arles. No cash prize amount specified. No application fee.

Duration: 6–8 weeks residency in early 2027, followed by a solo summer 2027 exhibition

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Prix Art & Environnement (4th edition), awarded by Lee Ufan Arles and Maison Guerlain, supports an artist working at the intersection of art and the natural environment. The prize winner receives a funded 6–8 week production residency in Arles in early 2027, including production space, accommodation, and mentorship from Lee Ufan Arles staff, culminating in a solo summer 2027 exhibition. ELIGIBILITY: open to all artists of any discipline and nationality, with no age or degree requirements; candidates must demonstrate at least one solo exhibition and one or more group exhibitions. DEADLINE: 31 July 2026. APPLY via the Google Form linked from the Lee Ufan Arles website.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Women Photograph + Leica Project Grants 2026 (Documentary Photography)
item.description:
Organisation: Women Photograph (supported by Leica USA)

Location: Remote (international)

Award: Three grants of USD 10,000 each (USD 30,000 total). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based (new or in-progress photography projects).

Deadline: Closed 30 Jun 2026

Women Photograph + Leica Project Grants 2026 awards three grants of USD 10,000 each to support documentary photography projects. ELIGIBILITY: open to women, nonbinary, transgender, and intersex documentary photographers of any nationality; no membership required; no restrictions on camera equipment or career stage. Funding covers hard costs of reporting, photographers' creative fees, and any other expenses that support the production of new or in-progress still photography projects. Not intended for books, exhibitions, or workshops. Grantees retain full work ownership. One submission per applicant; applications in English (Google Translate acceptable); any use of generative AI must be disclosed. Applications opened 20 May 2026; deadline 30 June 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. Apply at https://app.picter.com/contests/bad7ea97-7b0d-4a9c-9e85-38e73529d62b/start.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Async Museum: Open Call for Web-Based Art 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Async Museum

Location: Online (independent digital space); open to artists worldwide.

Award: Exhibition and visibility opportunity, NOT a paid grant: selected web-based artworks are presented in Async Museum's independent online space. No artist fee is stated.

Duration: Rolling open call (exhibition in Async Museum's online space)

Deadline: 01 Dec 2026

Async Museum is an independent digital space dedicated to web art with a distinctive point of view, existing to discover artists who use the web as their primary medium and to recognise their work as a new aesthetic. Its core philosophy is that the web browser is a twenty-first century canvas. Async Museum is officially seeking submissions of web-based artworks, welcoming both established practitioners and emerging voices who are pushing the boundaries of what the next generation of art can be. NOTE: this is an exhibition opportunity for web-based art rather than a paid grant; no artist fee is stated. To share your practice and submit your work, visit https://asyncmuseum.com/submit. Deadline: 1 December 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: ROTLICHT Festival for Analog Photography 2026: Open Call (Vienna, Austria)
item.description:
Organisation: Zigutamve – Association for Contemporary Photography

Location: Academy of Fine Arts, Vienna, Austria

Award: EUR 250 cash award per selected winner + EUR 150 travel allowance. Grand Prix winner receives an additional EUR 250, a solo exhibition, and a free hotel stay. Submission fee: EUR 20 per project.

Duration: Exhibition at Academy of Fine Arts Vienna; announcement August 2026, public reveal September 2026.

Deadline: Closed 30 Jun 2026

ROTLICHT Festival for Analog Photography 2026, organised by Zigutamve – Association for Contemporary Photography, selects 20 projects for exhibition at the Academy of Fine Arts Vienna. Theme: 'Anthropocene: Hybrid Realities' – exploring the tension between human ecological dominance and environmental fragility. Each selected winner receives: exhibition in a 1,300+ m² venue, publication in the official festival catalogue (2 copies), EUR 250 cash award, EUR 150 travel allowance, festival pass, and free registration for Photodays Rovinj 2027. Grand Prix winner additionally receives a solo exhibition, an extra EUR 250, a free hotel stay, and a catalogue cover feature. ELIGIBILITY: open to any individual aged 16 or older, at any career stage, internationally; submissions must include at least one analog photographic process; works must have been taken within the last 5 years (2021-2026); no AI-generated or AI-enhanced work (unless deliberately thematic). Files must be JPEG, max 5 MB, minimum 2000px on longest side, 300 dpi. Jury selects winners by late July 2026. Deadline: 30 June 2026. NOTE: EUR 20 submission fee per project applies. Apply at https://www.rotlicht-festival.at/open-call-submission/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Prix Ibn Khaldoun – Léopold Sédar Senghor Translation Prize 2026 (OIF / ALECSO)
item.description:
Organisation: Organisation internationale de la Francophonie (OIF) and Arab League Educational, Cultural and Scientific Organization (ALECSO)

Location: International (Francophone zone and Arab world)

Award: EUR 10,000 (funded equally by OIF and ALECSO). No application fee.

Duration: Prize (one-time award).

Deadline: Closed 30 Jun 2026

The Ibn Khaldoun – Léopold Sédar Senghor Translation Prize (19th edition, 2026) is awarded jointly by the Organisation internationale de la Francophonie (OIF) and ALECSO to recognise excellence in literary and humanities/social sciences translation between French and Arabic in either direction. Cash award: EUR 10,000. ELIGIBILITY: open to individual translators, universities and higher education institutions, research centres, national associations and unions, and publishing houses from the Arab world and Francophone space. Works must be literary or humanities/social sciences texts translated between French and Arabic (either direction), published within 3 years prior to the application year, and must be a first translation of the original. Self-published or photocopied works excluded. One work maximum per candidate. Applications submitted exclusively electronically; required materials: CV, presentation of translation work, copies of both translated and original texts. Deadline: 30 June 2026. Apply at https://appelsaprojets.francophonie.org/fr/applications/soutien-a-la-circulation-des-biens-et-mobilite-des-artistes.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: eidolon Grant 2026 (Vernacular Photography)
item.description:
Organisation: eidolon

Location: International; open worldwide.

Award: A total of EUR 25,000 to be distributed across two application categories. No application fee.

Duration: Annual grant; supported projects contribute to the institution's program in the coming year

Deadline: 30 Sep 2026

The eidolon Grant is an international programme, presented annually, for projects that explore, promote and conserve vernacular photography. It is open to artists, academics, professionals, researchers, collectors and vernacular photography enthusiasts whose past work and proposed project is centred around the image heritage of everyday photography. The grant aims to identify phenomena, collections, histories, practices and trends within vernacular photography, offering new interpretations and analyses, and thematising both photographic heritages and contemporary photographic practices. Applications can be made under two categories, with a total of EUR 25,000 to be distributed. Each chosen project will contribute to the enrichment of eidolon's program in the coming year. The deadline for submission is 30 September 2026 at 11:59 p.m. Apply via https://everydayphotography.org/centre/grant-2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Cooper Prize 2026 (16th Street Foundation and Company 16, Melbourne)
item.description:
Organisation: 16th Street Foundation and Company 16

Location: Melbourne, Australia (international eligibility)

Award: AUD 20,000 prize to winner, plus consideration for a full commission and production of the winning work. All finalists receive professionally directed public readings by professional actors. No application fee.

Duration: Prize (one-time award); winning work may proceed to full commission and production.

Deadline: Closed 19 Jun 2026

The Cooper Prize 2026 is an international playwriting prize administered by 16th Street Foundation and Company 16 in Melbourne, Australia. AUD 20,000 is awarded to the winning playwright, plus consideration for a full commission and professional production. All finalists receive professionally directed public readings by professional actors. Open internationally to published and unpublished playwrights at any career stage with no geographic restrictions. Submissions must respond to thematic prompts drawn from Victor Frankl's 'Man's Search for Meaning' and Dostoevsky's 'The Brothers Karamazov', exploring themes including doubt versus faith, the meaning of suffering, and the power of truth. Application requires: (1) a one-page document covering motivation for writing, issues to explore, philosophical perspective or worldview, and a synopsis or treatment; (2) ten pages of the play manuscript. Judging panel led by Iain Sinclair and Harry Haynes. Deadline: 19 June 2026. Apply via JotForm at https://form.jotform.com/260408855140858.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: AFAC: Music Grant 2026 (Arab Fund for Arts and Culture)
item.description:
Organisation: Arab Fund for Arts and Culture (AFAC)

Location: Arab region (open to individuals from the 22 Arab League states regardless of residence; institutions local, regional, and international working on Arab arts and culture)

Award: Annual grants of up to USD 25,000 for individuals and teams, and up to USD 35,000 for collectives and institutions. An artist or institutional fee of up to 30% of the total grant is an allowable expense. No application fee.

Duration: Projects to be completed within a maximum of two years from signature of the grant agreement

Deadline: Closed 19 Jun 2026

AFAC's Music grant supports music production, performances, collaborations, album recordings, music-related podcasts, and festivals. The program provides annual grants of up to USD 25,000 for individuals and teams, and up to USD 35,000 for collectives and institutions. WHO CAN APPLY: AFAC welcomes proposals from individuals from Arab countries (the 22 member states of the Arab League), regardless of place of residence, citizenship, or ethnic and national identification; it also accepts proposals from local, regional, and international institutions and organizations (artistic and cultural institutions, galleries, venues, and both nonprofit and for-profit entities) whose projects relate to arts and culture from the Arab region. INELIGIBLE: AFAC board, staff, their business partners or family, and current-year reader and juror committee members for the categories they evaluate; recipients of grants in two consecutive years are ineligible for the next two consecutive years; and applicants with a current open grant. AFAC launches two open calls a year; the second call covers Cinema, Music, and Training & Regional Events grants. Each applicant may submit only one application per open call. WHAT IT COVERS: project production including research and development, covering project-related expenses such as materials, space or equipment rentals, and fees for artistic and technical labor. An artist or institutional fee of up to 30% of the total grant is allowable. AFAC does not offer mobility grants, though project-related travel may be covered, and does not fund retroactively. PAYMENT: for grants above USD 10,000, applicants must show proof of at least 50% of the remaining project budget secured; payments are staged (50/35/15 for grants above USD 10,000, 70/30 for grants below). SELECTION: a grants management completeness review, then an independent readers committee, then an independent jury committee that reaches the final decision. AFAC does not own any material or moral rights to supported projects. Applications are accepted only through the online forms (separate forms for individuals, teams, collectives, and institutions). TIMELINE: deadline 19 June 2026 at 5:00 PM +3 GMT (Beirut time); announcement of selected projects 16 November 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Future Generation Art Prize 2027 (PinchukArtCentre)
item.description:
Organisation: Victor Pinchuk Foundation (PinchukArtCentre)

Location: Kyiv, Ukraine (exhibition of shortlisted artists at PinchukArtCentre in Spring 2027)

Award: Main prize US$100,000 ($60,000 cash + $40,000 investment in the winner's practice). Up to 5 Special Prizes share a total amount of US$20,000 for supporting projects that develop their artistic practice. No application fee.

Duration: Biannual prize cycle (shortlist Sept 2026; exhibition and award ceremony Spring 2027)

Deadline: Closed 28 Jun 2026

Biannual global contemporary art prize from the Victor Pinchuk Foundation (PinchukArtCentre, Kyiv), recognising and supporting a future generation of artists. The main winner receives US$100,000 ($60,000 cash plus $40,000 invested in their practice); up to five special prizes share a total of US$20,000 for projects developing the artist's practice. Shortlisted artists exhibit at PinchukArtCentre in Spring 2027, with the award ceremony following. ELIGIBILITY: open to all artists aged 35 or younger worldwide, with no restrictions on gender, nationality, race or artistic medium; former Prize winners are not eligible to enter again but other previous applicants may re-apply. APPLY through the open call online. A selection committee reviews applications and selects up to twenty artists for the exhibition; in addition, 300 correspondent art experts worldwide nominate two to five candidates each. Applications accepted 11 May - 28 June 2026; shortlist announced 14 September 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Biafarin: BOAPS - Summer 2026 (international online art prize)
item.description:
Organisation: Biafarin

Location: Online (Canadian organisation; worldwide submissions and online exhibition)

Award: Cash grants per artist: Major Grant CAD 500 (1 artist); Merit Grant CAD 250 each (4 artists, total CAD 1,000 pool); Recognition Grant CAD 100 each (10 artists, total CAD 1,000 pool). NOTE: submission is free, but SELECTED ARTISTS PAY a USD 50 selection fee (USD 40 early bird) to participate in the exhibition. Money-back guarantee on the selection fee until exhibition opening day.

Duration: Online group exhibition 1 Jul - 30 Sep 2026 (3 months); up to 10 selected for separate 2-month online solo exhibitions

Deadline: Closed 09 Jun 2026

Seasonal international art prize from Biafarin (a Canadian artist-management platform), running a juried online group exhibition with cash grants, magazine/catalogue features, ARTSY presentation, and up to 10 solo online exhibitions. CASH GRANTS (modest): Major Grant CAD 500 (1 artist); Merit Grant CAD 250 each (4 artists); Recognition Grant CAD 100 each (10 artists). Plus up to 10 Honorable Mention certificates and various promotion benefits. ELIGIBILITY: open to all visual artists worldwide aged 18+, any 2D/3D medium (painting, drawing, illustration, printmaking, photography, digital art, fiber, sculpture, mixed media); originals only, no copies/derivatives. SUBMISSION: free of charge. NOTE ON FEE: only SELECTED artists pay a fixed USD 50 selection fee (USD 40 early-bird) to participate in the exhibition; most selected artists pay this fee and do not receive a cash grant. Money-back guarantee on the selection fee until the official exhibition opening day. Up to 10 artworks per artist; max 2 may be selected. Deadline 9 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Belgrade Art Studio: Virtual Art Lab (12 Weeks, All Media)
item.description:
Organisation: Belgrade Art Studio

Location: Remote (online; English-language program; worldwide)

Award: PAID PROGRAM (artist pays): EUR 675 program fee. NOT a grant: no stipend, no bursary, no production budget. Includes 12 one-hour 1-on-1 mentor sessions, a final online viewing/discussion, and artist interview/promotion. No separate application fee.

Duration: 12 weeks / 3 months; weekly 1-hour individual sessions (Zoom or Google Meet); not full-time

Deadline: Rolling / undated

PAID 12-week online mentorship program from Belgrade Art Studio: each artist works one-on-one with a dedicated artist-mentor through weekly 1-hour Zoom/Google Meet sessions, aimed at artists at a turning point in their practice (stuck, seeking new directions, experimenting with unfamiliar media including AI). Hybrid seminar/workshop format supporting both physical and online presentation, plus curatorial thinking, writing, interviews, and documentation. Closes with an online viewing and moderated discussion of the works produced. ELIGIBILITY: open worldwide to artists and creatives across visual arts, music, theatre, dance, film, writing, design, photography, digital/interactive art, games, fashion, architecture, and curation. English-language program (ability to collaborate in English preferred but accommodations possible). EXPECTATIONS: artists must treat the program as they would an in-person residency (dedicated time for research/creation/reflection), submit in-progress material for social-media exposure, participate in an artist interview, and refrain from hate speech or discriminatory content. APPLY by emailing CV, summary, and portfolio (links accepted) to artlab@belgradeartstudio.com. Rolling intake (no deadline). Selected artists are announced and contacted by email. COST: EUR 675 to participate — this is a paid offering, not a funded residency.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: .ART Award 2026 (Global Art Prize)
item.description:
Organisation: .ART (with Whitewall Magazine, Chateau du Fresne, Anfitrion)

Location: Hybrid mobility (Grand Prize cash + residencies in France and Spain)

Award: Grand Prize: USD 15,000 cash. Artist Residency Award (France): one month at Chateau du Fresne, near Paris. Artist Residency Award (Spain): residency at Anfitrion in Marbella. Publication Award: editorial coverage in Whitewall Magazine (campaign media partner). .ART Domain Award: a USD 10,000 premium .ART domain name. No application fee mentioned (submission is via a .ART domain that documents the practice).

Duration: One-off open call; winners revealed during Art Basel Miami on 3 December 2026; residency prizes scheduled separately

Deadline: 01 Nov 2026

Global art prize celebrating how art is made, open to artists worldwide working in any medium or art form. Instead of a traditional application form, artists submit a digital presence (website, portfolio or social profile) linked through a .ART domain that documents identity, process and work as one continuous record. PRIZES: Grand Prize (USD 15,000 cash); Artist Residency Award France (one month at Chateau du Fresne, near Paris); Artist Residency Award Spain (residency at Anfitrion, Marbella); Publication Award (editorial coverage in Whitewall Magazine, the campaign's media partner); .ART Domain Award (USD 10,000 premium .ART domain name). ELIGIBILITY: individual artists and artist collectives from all backgrounds (cultures, ethnicities, gender identities, abilities); 18 or older at time of submission; open to artists from all geographic regions with no restrictions on nationality or place of residence. Multiple entries permitted, as long as each submission (single artwork, series or overall practice) is presented on or linked to a SEPARATE .ART domain. NOTE: a .ART domain is required for submission; this is the de facto entry mechanism. ANNOUNCEMENT: winners revealed during Art Basel Miami on 3 December 2026. DEADLINE: 1 November 2026. APPLY via the .ART Award site.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Naomi Anolic Early Career Jewish Visual Arts Award 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Anolic Family Award

Location: Online application (open to early-career Jewish artists)

Award: $1,000 award for one early-career Jewish artist. No application fee.

Duration: One-off award; project supported within the award year, with a final artist statement and visual documentation due at year end

Deadline: Closed 01 Jun 2026

Annual award providing momentum for early-career Jewish visual artists for a specific project. The 2026 cycle focuses on the fine arts: painting, drawing, sculpture and mixed media. Recipients must provide a brief artist statement and visual documentation of the supported work at year end, and must credit the Naomi Anolic Early Career Jewish Visual Arts Award in any exhibition or publication of the supported work. ELIGIBILITY: active early-career Jewish artists aged 25 to 35; students may not apply. APPLY with a max 600-word proposal (why you're applying, why you're early-career, project/focus, how the award will help, how Judaism informs and is realised in your work - overt Jewish imagery is not required), 10 jpg images (max 1024px longest side, max 4 MB each), an image list, an up-to-2-page CV/resume, and an artist website (or social-media link if no site). Award announcement by September 2026. Questions: info@anolicfamilyaward.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Arte Laguna Prize 2026 (21st Edition)
item.description:
Organisation: Arte Laguna (Venice)

Location: Venice, Italy (finalist exhibition at Arsenale Nord)

Award: EUR 10,000 main prize plus additional FOMAS prizes (EUR 7,000 / 4,000 / 3,000) and a finalist exhibition at the Venice Arsenale Nord. Entry fee approximately EUR 97 to EUR 122.

Duration: One-off prize

Deadline: Closed 30 Jun 2026

International Arte Laguna Prize, open to artists across many media including dedicated digital art and video sections, with a finalist exhibition at the Venice Arsenale Nord. ELIGIBILITY: individual artists of all ages and nationalities worldwide. NOTE: an entry fee applies (roughly EUR 97 to EUR 122), but the prize awards substantial cash. Apply via the Arte Laguna Prize page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: PhotoVogue Global Open Call 2026: Brave New Visions - Creativity as Rebellion
item.description:
Organisation: PhotoVogue (Conde Nast)

Location: Online (global)

Award: $12,000 in total grants split across three artists: a $6,000 Outstanding Vision Grant, a $4,000 Vision Grant, and a $2,000 Rising Voice Grant (emerging), plus presentation at the next PhotoVogue Festival, potential publication across Vogue's global editions, and PhotoVogue Virtual Portfolio Reviews. Free to submit.

Duration: One-off open call

Deadline: 11 Sep 2026

PhotoVogue's global open call invites photographers and video makers to use image-making as a form of rebellion - to challenge indifference, disrupt conventions and expand visual storytelling. It asks not for a theme but for a position in relation to the world. Open to photography, video and multimedia projects across all genres (fashion, documentary, portraiture, fine art, experimental). Three artists share $12,000 in grants ($6,000 Outstanding Vision, $4,000 Vision, $2,000 Rising Voice for an emerging artist), with festival presentation, potential Vogue publication and portfolio reviews. ELIGIBILITY: open to all artists worldwide aged 18+; submissions are free; previous PhotoVogue applicants may submit a new project. SUBMIT: a series of up to 15 images, or images plus video for multimedia, and/or a 60-second trailer, via Picter. IMPORTANT: AI-generated works are NOT eligible. Open 14 May to 11 September 2026 (11:59 PM CET).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Beautiful Bizarre Art Prize 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Beautiful Bizarre Magazine

Location: Worldwide (exhibition in San Francisco, USA)

Award: Grand Prize $10,000 plus category awards (e.g. Imaginative Realism $5,000) and a dedicated Digital Art Award and Emerging Artist Award; $76,000+ total in cash and prizes. NOTE: $40 entry fee per submission.

Duration: Annual prize (exhibition follows)

Deadline: Closed 17 Jul 2026

International, non-acquisitive art prize across seven categories including a dedicated Digital Art Award, with cash prizes and a San Francisco gallery exhibition. Grand Prize is $10,000, with category awards and over $76,000 in total cash and prizes. ELIGIBILITY: open internationally with no geographic restriction; all static media; includes an Emerging Artist Award. NOTE: there is a $40 entry fee per submission.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: FILE NOT FOUND: Open Call for Archive Residencies
item.description:
Organisation: Goethe-Institut

Location: On-site at ten cultural archives around the world; final exhibition at BLMK Cottbus, Germany

Award: EUR 4,000 (EUR 2,000 artist fee + EUR 2,000 production budget). Travel and accommodation covered up to EUR 2,000 for one person.

Duration: On-site residency of 2 weeks to 2 months, scheduled between August 2026 and May 2027

Deadline: Closed 07 Jun 2026

Open call to participate in FILE NOT FOUND, a project exploring the risks and challenges archives face as sites of cultural memory, resistant knowledge and alternative perspectives under growing political pressure. Ten practitioners - artists, thinkers, activists, theatre makers, dancers, musicians and more - will be selected for residencies at ten cultural archives around the world. On site, residents engage with existing archival practices, explore both safe and not-so-safe spaces, investigate traditional and experimental methods of archiving, and ideally develop artistically subversive approaches that strengthen the resilience of archives against external influence. ELIGIBILITY: artists worldwide, all disciplines. Applicants review the ten participating archives and select the one they wish to apply to. The on-site residency runs 2 weeks to 2 months between August 2026 and May 2027. OUTCOME: an artwork plus a residency report, with a group exhibition at BLMK Cottbus, Germany in autumn 2027. Working language English.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: PinchukArtCentre: Future Generation Art Prize 2026
item.description:
Organisation: PinchukArtCentre / Victor Pinchuk Foundation

Location: Worldwide (online application; exhibition in Kyiv and at Venice Biennale 2027)

Award: Main Prize: $100,000 ($60,000 cash + $40,000 invested in production). Up to five Special Prizes of $20,000 each.

Duration: Per artist; exhibition cycle runs August 2027

Deadline: Closed 14 May 2026

Biennial international prize for individual artists or collectives worldwide aged 18-35. No application fee. Open to all visual-arts media, including video, installation, performance, photography. Excluded: applicants with Russian or Belarusian citizenship/residency, and former winners. Shortlist announced late November 2026; shortlisted artists are exhibited at PinchukArtCentre in Kyiv and at a collateral event at the Venice Biennale 2027 (exhibition opens August 2027). Of the $40,000 production investment for the main winner, the funds are tied to producing new work. Strong fit for an artist working at the intersection of installation, video and critical AI/surveillance themes (an AI/drones installation would qualify as a video/installation submission). Tight age window (must be 35 or under at deadline); the prize is positioned as career-launching for younger practitioners.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: VIA Art Fund: Artistic Production Grant (Fall 2026 cycle)
item.description:
Organisation: VIA Art Fund

Location: Worldwide (projects exhibited beyond museum walls, in the public realm, or in non-traditional exhibition environments)

Award: $25,000 to $100,000 per project

Duration: Per project (must start after 15 December 2026)

Deadline: Closed 21 May 2026

Production grants for newly commissioned works of visual art exhibited beyond museum walls, in the public realm, or in non-traditional exhibition environments. Grants are awarded to projects that best exemplify VIA's three core values of Artistic Production, Thought Leadership, and Public Engagement. Two-stage application process: Letter of Inquiry due 21 May 2026; invitations to submit a full application sent 24 June 2026 (by invitation only); full application deadline for invited applicants 23 July 2026; grant award notification November 2026. Projects must start after 15 December 2026. VIA runs Fall and Spring cycles each year; Fall 2026 is the current open call.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Boiler Room Broadcast Lab #14 (2026)
item.description:
Organisation: Boiler Room

Location: Worldwide (project streamed on Boiler Room channels)

Award: Up to GBP 10,000 to produce the project, plus in-kind support from Boiler Room and a full release across Boiler Room's channels

Duration: Production timeline TBD by project; output is a streamed broadcast on Boiler Room.

Deadline: Closed 17 May 2026

Open call for individual musicians, collectives, and curators to pitch a project to be streamed on Boiler Room. The fund looks to platform overlooked scenes and spaces, innovative programming concepts that prioritise underrepresented artists, marginalised communities, and forward-thinking collaborations. While Boiler Room's archive is heavily DJ-focused, applicants are encouraged to explore live performance, instrumentation, and non-club genres. Winning application receives up to GBP 10,000 to bring the project to life, plus additional in-kind support and a full release on Boiler Room's channels. Deadline: Sunday 17 May at 23:59 BST. This is the 14th edition of Broadcast Lab.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Olympic Heritage: Artists in Residence 2026
item.description:
Organisation: 3-2-1 Qatar Olympic & Sports Museum (QOSM) and The Olympic Museum, Lausanne

Location: Doha, Qatar OR La Tour-de-Peilz, Switzerland (one residency in each location)

Award: Round-trip flights + accommodation + living expenses allowance + mentorship + QAR 30,000 / €8,000 acquisition fee for resulting artwork (museums' permanent collections) + participation in international exhibition as part of the Cultural Olympiad (including LA 2028)

Duration: 3 months (1 September to 1 December 2026)

Deadline: Closed 30 Apr 2026

Joint residency by the 3-2-1 Qatar Olympic & Sports Museum and The Olympic Museum in Lausanne for digital and immersive media artists to critically engage with sports culture and Olympic Legacy. Residents get exclusive access to museum collections, mentorship and expert support. The Doha residency is hosted at QOSM; the Swiss residency is at La Becque Artist Residence in La Tour-de-Peilz. Open to multi-disciplinary artists worldwide aged 18 to 35 (as of 31.12.2026) whose practice involves a significant element of digital art (any new media form, possibly combined with physical media). Free entry. Submit in English via the official online form by 30 April 2026 at 23:59 (GMT+1).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Creativity Pioneers Fund 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Creativity Pioneers Fund (Moleskine Foundation)

Location: Anywhere (legally registered nonprofits only)

Award: €5,000 unearmarked micro-grant

Duration: Starting point for long-term collaboration (mentorship, peer exchange, follow-on support)

Deadline: Closed 24 Apr 2026

Unearmarked €5,000 micro-grant for cultural and creative nonprofits advancing bold, unconventional practices toward a more just, inclusive and equitable world. Beyond the grant, recipients gain access to in-kind support, mentorship, learning opportunities, peer-to-peer exchange, visibility and networking. Open only to active, legally registered nonprofit organisations (not individuals; an organisational bank account is required), regardless of geographical location. Looking for organisations that place creativity at the core of their mission, prioritise disadvantaged and/or marginalised communities (primarily youth aged 16 to 27), and focus on long-term systemic change with a local-yet-globally-relevant perspective. Not interested in art-for-art's-sake, conventional NGO approaches, or amateur/improvised initiatives. The 2026 call ran 30 March to 24 April 2026, 17:00 CEST. Award decisions announced in September; agreements and disbursement by December 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The 2026 Lumen Prize
item.description:
Organisation: The Lumen Prize

Location: Global

Award: 3 x $5,000 category awards + $15,000 Gold Award

Duration: One-off prize

Deadline: Closed 23 May 2026

International art prize across three categories, each with a $5,000 award: Legacy Futures (works engaging past, present and future, examining our relationship to technology, culture and time); Systems & Structures (works examining or critically engaging with cultural, ecological, technological and social systems via code, data, networks, biological or environmental processes, institutional frameworks etc., e.g. misinformation, machine vision, human-machine relations); and Experiential Innovation (works prioritising experience via participation, presence, sensation or environment, including live coding, performance, interactive installations, experiential VR/AR and cross-modal practices). The Jury also selects one Gold Award Winner across all categories, awarded $15,000 in 2026. Two-stage judging: an International Selectors Committee curates a longlist of finalists, then a Jury Panel selects the winners. Standard entry fee $45.00; submissions close 23 May 2026 at 19:00. If the entry fee is a barrier, contact info@lumenprize.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CIFRA Award: RestArt Reality (Open Call)
item.description:
Organisation: CIFRA

Location: International (online submissions)

Award: 5 winners x €1,000

Duration: One-time award

Deadline: Closed 08 May 2026

Open call for any form of digital art submittable as a video, around the theme 'RestArt Reality': screen-native works engaging with pseudo-evidence, invented archives, alternative chronologies, reconstructions and 'false' testimonies. Five winners each receive €1,000.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Open Call: Netstalgia (Not Acceptable) - Error 406
item.description:
Organisation: Error 417: Expectation Fail

Location: Online

Award: €1,500 to €7,000 per project

Duration: Project-based (work delivery deadline 30 October 2026)

Deadline: Closed 04 May 2026

Open call for artists, curators and collectives worldwide to propose projects that take a clear-eyed view of internet histories and explore how they can open pathways toward more equitable futures. The brief: what protocols, networks, archives and shared conditions are needed to build the internet we actually want? Respond to netstalgia with error 406 not acceptable. Jury: Chia Amisola, Olia Lialina, Peter Sunde and Vladan Joler. Project deadline 4 May 2026 (23:59 CEST); work delivery 30 October 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
